ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "iso3_code" TEXT /* ISO3 code of the country */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region the country belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country the metric belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the recorded metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the environmental metric */,
  "metric_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Data quality score or flag */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_metrics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_metrics_metric_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_type_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the environmental metric (e.g., CO2 emissions, air quality index) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the metric (e.g., air, water, land) */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the metric */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Primary source of the metric data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the user (e.g., university, organization) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the metric */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES environmental_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data or description of the report */,
  "metric_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric type the report is based on */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region the report is based on */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path or URL of the report if stored externally */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_metric_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_type_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id")
)
